mission.title: kubeflow: Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow
mission.description: Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow. This issue affects 4+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
There's a lot of good jupyter images already out there.

For example #767 discusses a Jupyter image with swift support for TensorFlow.

We'd also like to support images with R.

As discussed in this [

steps.title: Apply the fix for Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow
steps.description: Modified entrypoint command for jupyter images to launch jupyter on boot. --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/kubeflow/kubeflow/2517) ```yaml container.Env = append(container.Env, corev1.EnvVar{ Name: "NB_PREFIX", Value: "/notebook/" + instance.Namespace + "/" + instance.Name, }) ```

steps.title: Confirm Make arbitrary Jupyter images work with Kubeflow is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: The root cause is: Modified entrypoint command for jupyter images to launch jupyter on boot. --- This change is [](https://reviewable.io/reviews/kubeflow/kubeflow/2517). This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
# allow apache2 to use the NB_PREFIX provided by kubeflow
# note: we strip off any trailing / for consistency
shopt -s extglob;
echo "export NB_PREFIX_NO_SLASH=${NB_PREFIX%%+(/)}" >> /etc/apache2/envvars

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
